title: Sign in to your study permit account - Canada.ca
content:
## Studying in Canada : Sign in to your account
Select what you applied for to find your account.
Haven't applied yet? Find out if you need a study permit and how to apply .

## The Student Direct Stream is closed
The Student Direct Stream ended on November 8, 2024, at 2 p.m. ET. Applications submitted under this stream on or
        after this date and time will be processed as regular
            study permit applications .

## New provincial/territorial attestation letter exemptions for study permit applications
Students may need to include a provincial attestation letter (PAL) or territorial attestation letter (TAL) from the province or territory where they plan to study when they apply for a study permit.
If your application is already in progress (but hasn’t been submitted), check the list of PAL/TAL exemptions to see if you need to include one.
You used one of these 2 accounts to apply for a study permit.

### IRCC Portal: Option 1 of 2

### IRCC secure account: Option 2 of 2
Go to IRCC secure account
If you’re not sure which account you used to apply , try signing in to both or check your email.

## Study permit (with a family member who needed a work permit)
You used the IRCC secure account to apply for a study permit (if you applied with a family member who applied for a work permit).

## Study permit extension
You used the IRCC secure account to apply to extend your study permit.
You used the IRCC secure account to change your school.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
